Usage:
  AMSMigrate [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level for logging [default: Warning]
  -l, --log-directory <log-directory>                                    The directory where the logs are written. Defaults to the temporary directory [default: /tmp/]
  -s, --subscription <subscription> (REQUIRED)                           The azure subscription to use
  -g, --resource-group <resource-group> (REQUIRED)                       The resource group of the account beging migrated
  -n, --account-name <account-name> (REQUIRED)                           The target Azure Media Services or Azure Storage Account being migrated.
  -c, --cloud-type <AWS|Azure|Custom|GCP|Local>                          The destination cloud you are migrating to.
                                                                         For Azure refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/azure.identity.defaultazurecredential?view=azure-dotnet
                                                                         For AWS refer to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-net/v3/developer-guide/creds-locate.html
                                                                         For GCP refer to https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials
                                                                         Depending on the type of authentcation you may have to set some environment variables. [default: Azure]
  -d, --daemon-mode                                                      Run in daemon mode and do not use ASCII graphics. [default: False]
  --version                                                              Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                         Show help and usage information

Commands:
  analyze     Analyze assets for migration and generate report.
              Example(s):
              amsmigrate analyze -s <subscriptionid> -g <resourcegroup> -n <account>
              This will analyze the given media account and produce a summary report.
  assets      Migrate Assets
              Examples:
              amsmigrate assets -s <subscription id> -g <resource group> -n <account name> -a <storage account> -t path-template
              This migrates the assets to a different storage account in your subscription.
  storage     Directly migrate the assets from the storage account.
              Doesn't require the Azure media services to be running.
  keys        Migrate asset encryption keys
  liveevents  Migrate Live Events
  transforms  Migrate Transforms

Usage:
  AMSMigrate analyze [options]

Options:
  -b, --batch-size <batch-size>                                          Batch size for parallel processing. [default: 1]
  -t, --analysis-type <Detailed|Report|Summary>                          The kind of analysis to do.
                                                                         Summary - Summary of migration
                                                                         Detailed - A detailed classification of assets,
                                                                         Report - A migration report [default: Summary]
  -v, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level for logging [default: Warning]
  -l, --log-directory <log-directory>                                    The directory where the logs are written. Defaults to the temporary directory [default: /tmp/]
  -s, --subscription <subscription> (REQUIRED)                           The azure subscription to use
  -g, --resource-group <resource-group> (REQUIRED)                       The resource group of the account beging migrated
  -n, --account-name <account-name> (REQUIRED)                           The target Azure Media Services or Azure Storage Account being migrated.
  -c, --cloud-type <AWS|Azure|Custom|GCP|Local>                          The destination cloud you are migrating to.
                                                                         For Azure refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/azure.identity.defaultazurecredential?view=azure-dotnet
                                                                         For AWS refer to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-net/v3/developer-guide/creds-locate.html
                                                                         For GCP refer to https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials
                                                                         Depending on the type of authentcation you may have to set some environment variables. [default: Azure]
  -d, --daemon-mode                                                      Run in daemon mode and do not use ASCII graphics. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                         Show help and usage information

Usage:
  AMSMigrate assets [options]

Options:
  --created-after <created-after>                                        Query entities created after given date.
  --created-before <created-before>                                      Query entities created before given date.
  -f, --filter <filter>                                                  An ODATA condition to filter the resources.
                                                                         e.g.: "name eq 'asset1'" to match an asset with name 'asset1'.
                                                                         Visit https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/media-services/latest/filter-order-page-entities-how-to for more information.
  -m, --mark-complete                                                    Mark completed assets by writing metadata on the container [default: True]
  --skip-migrated                                                        Skip assets that have been migrated already. [default: True]
  --packager <Ffmpeg|None|Shaka>                                         The packager to use. [default: Shaka]
  --working-dir <working-dir>                                            The working directory to use migration. [default: /tmp/AMSMigrate]
  --copy-nonstremable                                                    Copy non-stremable assets (Assets without .ism file) as is. [default: True]
  -b, --batch-size <batch-size>                                          Batch size for parallel processing. [default: 1]
  -o, --storage-path <storage-path> (REQUIRED)                           The output storage account or path to upload the migrated assets.
                                                                         This is specific to the cloud you are migrating to.
                                                                         e.g: For Azure specify the storage account name or the URL <https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net>
  -y, --overwrite                                                        Overwrite the files in the destination. [default: False]
  -t, --path-template <path-template>                                    Path template to determine the final path in the storage where files are uploaded.
                                                                         Can use ${AssetName} ${AssetId} ${ContainerName} or ${LocatorId}.
                                                                         e.g., videos/${AssetName} will upload to a container named 'videos' with path begining with the asset name. [default: ${AssetId}/]
  -a, --asset-names <asset-names>                                        Select specific asset(s) by name. You can specify multiple assets
  -v, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log level for logging [default: Warning]
  -l, --log-directory <log-directory>                                    The directory where the logs are written. Defaults to the temporary directory [default: /tmp/]
  -s, --subscription <subscription> (REQUIRED)                           The azure subscription to use
  -g, --resource-group <resource-group> (REQUIRED)                       The resource group of the account beging migrated
  -n, --account-name <account-name> (REQUIRED)                           The target Azure Media Services or Azure Storage Account being migrated.
  -c, --cloud-type <AWS|Azure|Custom|GCP|Local>                          The destination cloud you are migrating to.
                                                                         For Azure refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/azure.identity.defaultazurecredential?view=azure-dotnet
                                                                         For AWS refer to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-net/v3/developer-guide/creds-locate.html
                                                                         For GCP refer to https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials
                                                                         Depending on the type of authentcation you may have to set some environment variables. [default: Azure]
  -d, --daemon-mode                                                      Run in daemon mode and do not use ASCII graphics. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                         Show help and usage information
